topic: The '4 Refined' Predictions — Named and Examined
our_argument_summary: Section 6.5 names the dome's '4 Refined' predictions as W027 (Schumann elevation), W019 (NMP drift direction), W020 (Roaring 40s 500hPa), and W021 (Moon angular diameter). It argues all four produced results contradicting their original predictions — two showing the exact opposite — and the dome relabeled three as 'refined' and one as 'suspended' to avoid counting them as falsified. The section concludes this is why the dome claims '0 falsified' while having four wrong predictions.

holes_found.description: WRONG FOUR IDENTIFIED — internal contradiction with Section 6.6. Section 6.5 lists the dome's '4 Refined' as W027, W019, W020, W021. But our own Section 6.6 correctly identifies the dome's acknowledged 4 failures as W019, W020, W024, W027 — matching the poller's findings from the dome's predictions page ('4 predictions explicitly refined/falsified: W019, W020, W024, W027'). W024 (Polaris elevation excess) is one of the dome's 4, labeled FALSIFIED by the dome itself. W021 (Moon angular diameter) is SUSPENDED and NOT in the dome's 4-count denominator. Two adjacent sections of our review name different sets of predictions. A dome defender can trivially discredit Section 6.5 by pointing to this factual error.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace W021 with W024 in Section 6.5. Restructure the narrative: the dome's 4 acknowledged failures are 1 FALSIFIED (W024 — Polaris elevation) + 3 REFINED (W027, W019, W020). Then add W021 as a FIFTH failure the dome doesn't count at all (suspended, excluded from denominator). This actually makes our argument STRONGER — the dome has more uncounted failures than it admits.

holes_found.description: SECTION CLAIMS DOME SAYS '0 FALSIFIED' — BUT DOME EXPLICITLY FALSIFIED W024. Our text says 'The dome's tracking page reports "4 Refined" predictions alongside "0 Falsified."' But the poller data from the dome's wins page shows 'W024 (Polaris Elevation) explicitly falsified.' The dome acknowledges at least 1 falsification. If we claim '0 Falsified' and the dome's page actually says otherwise, we're the ones misreporting — exactly the kind of error that lets defenders dismiss our entire review as sloppy.
holes_found.recommendation: Verify the exact language on the dome's current tracking page. If the dome's 4 include 1 falsified + 3 refined, update our text accordingly. The argument still works — the dome acknowledged 1 failure honestly but hid 3 others behind 'refined' — plus W021 (suspended) is entirely uncounted.

holes_found.description: FAIL-005 (W021 Moon angular diameter) geometry claim is imprecise. We say 'the dome's own H(r) firmament curve produces values incompatible with this prediction — the model's geometry cannot produce the claimed variation.' This is misleading. At moon altitude 2,534 km, the dome's geometry would produce ~83% angular diameter variation from moonrise (distance ~15,000+ km) to transit (distance = 2,534 km). The model doesn't FAIL to produce >2% — it MASSIVELY overpredicts, by about 40x. The real problem is: (a) the >2% threshold is trivially easy to meet under dome geometry, making the 'prediction' meaningless as a test, and (b) the ~83% variation is not observed — the moon looks roughly the same size at all elevations. Our text says the geometry 'cannot produce' the variation, which is the opposite of the truth.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite to: 'The dome's geometry at 2,534 km moon altitude would produce roughly 80% angular diameter variation from moonrise to transit — 40× more than the >2% threshold, and utterly incompatible with observation. The moon appears approximately the same angular size at all elevations. The prediction was set at a threshold so low that the dome's own geometry trivially exceeds it, yet even this minimal claim cannot be confirmed because the effect simply doesn't exist.'

holes_found.description: FAIL-004 (W020 Roaring 40s 500hPa) lacks specific data. For FAIL-002 and FAIL-003, we provide clear quantitative outcomes (35% suppression, 5.5× ratio). For FAIL-004, we say only 'The prediction failed. The dome changed the prediction parameters after the fact.' No specific measurement, no threshold comparison. A defender can dismiss this as vague hand-waving while accepting the other three as legitimate criticism.
holes_found.recommendation: Source the specific 500hPa geopotential height data for the test period. What was the actual anomaly measured? How far was it from the ≥3% threshold? If the data isn't available in our files, flag this as needing analyst research.

holes_found.description: Missing credit: FAIL-001 (W024, Polaris elevation) is a case where the dome CORRECTLY followed scientific practice and labeled a failed prediction as falsified. Our section doesn't mention this at all. Acknowledging it would strengthen our credibility — we're not claiming the dome never admits failures, just that it does so inconsistently and insufficiently. As written, a defender can say 'they falsified W024 honestly — maybe they're honest about the other 3 being genuinely refined too.'
holes_found.recommendation: If W024 is added as one of the four (as it should be), note that this is the one case where the dome properly acknowledged a failure. Then contrast: 'One prediction was honestly falsified. Three others — which also produced outcomes contradicting their predictions — were labeled "refined" instead.'

holes_found.description: Title says '4 Refined' but text acknowledges one was 'suspended.' If corrected per above (using the dome's actual 4 = 3 refined + 1 falsified), the title should change to '4 Acknowledged Failures' or '3 Refined, 1 Falsified — Named and Examined.'
holes_found.recommendation: Update title to match the corrected content.

stronger_arguments.description: W021 (Moon angular diameter, suspended) should be presented as a FIFTH uncounted failure, separate from the dome's 4. This makes the argument stronger: the dome counts 4 failures in its denominator, but at least 5 exist (plus FAIL-006/007/008 for removed/suspended eclipse predictions). The real denominator should include all of these.

The 'prediction reversal' pattern in FAIL-002 (Schumann: predicted elevation, observed suppression, then adopted suppression as new prediction) is a textbook example of 'degenerative research programme' in Lakatos's framework. The model accommodates contradictory outcomes without generating new testable predictions. This philosophical framing would resonate with scientifically literate readers.
sources: Lakatos, I. (1978). The Methodology of Scientific Research Programmes.

stronger_arguments.description: Quantitative computation for W021: The dome's moon at 2,534 km altitude produces ~83% angular size variation from horizon to zenith. This is not observed — the moon looks approximately the same size. This makes W021 not just a suspended prediction but a geometric SELF-CONTRADICTION of the dome model. It belongs in Section 2 (Self-Contradictions) as well as here.
sources: Computed from dome parameters: moon_altitude=2534 km, observer-to-moonrise horizontal distance ~15,000 km on flat disc

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome model does have a legitimate point that short test windows can produce misleading results. A weekly prediction tested over 7 days has more noise than a long-term prediction. The dome's W-series predictions (registered 2026-03-12, test by 2026-03-19) had genuinely tight windows, and some failures in short windows don't necessarily invalidate the underlying long-term model prediction.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The defense fails for three reasons: (1) FAIL-002 (Schumann) and FAIL-003 (NMP drift) showed the OPPOSITE of what was predicted — not a marginal miss but a directional failure. A short window can explain missing a threshold, but not getting the sign wrong. (2) The dome chose these test windows voluntarily. You don't get to register specific, time-bounded predictions and then discard the results when they come back wrong. (3) Most critically, 'refined' means the dome REPLACED the failed prediction with a new one (often borrowed from standard physics), not that it improved the measurement. The underlying model prediction was wrong, not the test window.

text_fingerprint.h2_title: 6.5 The "4 Refined" Predictions — Named and Examined

citations_unchecked: No DOI or external citations in this section — all claims reference internal data files (uncounted-failures.json, dome tracking page)
citation_check.internal_consistency_check: FAILED — Section 6.5 lists {W027, W019, W020, W021} but Section 6.6 lists {W019, W020, W024, W027}. The poller data confirms Section 6.6 is correct.

deeper_analysis: This section has a fundamental factual error that creates an internal contradiction with Section 6.6. The dome's 4 acknowledged failures are W019, W020, W024, W027 — not W019, W020, W021, W027. W024 (Polaris elevation) was explicitly FALSIFIED by the dome; W021 (Moon angular diameter) was SUSPENDED and is not in the dome's count at all. This means: (a) our claim that the dome says '0 Falsified' is likely wrong — the dome falsified W024; (b) W021 is an additional uncounted failure beyond the dome's 4, making our case stronger once corrected; (c) two adjacent sections of our review give different lists of the 4 failures, which is exactly the kind of sloppy error we criticize the dome for making. The fix actually strengthens our argument: we go from '4 predictions hidden as refined' to '1 honestly falsified + 3 hidden as refined + at least 1 more quietly suspended and excluded from the count entirely.' Additionally, the W021 geometry analysis reveals a self-contradiction: the dome's moon at 2,534 km would produce ~83% angular size variation, not the modest >2% predicted, and this massive variation is not observed at all.
